title: 6.0 Solid Figures
description: Grade 10 Math Unit 6 — extremely difficult problems on surface area and volume of prisms, cylinders, pyramids, cones, spheres, frustums, and composed solids.

questions.question: A right prism has a base shaped as a rhombus with diagonals of lengths 12 cm and 16 cm. The lateral surface area of the prism is 600 cm². Find the volume of the prism.
The total surface area of a right circular cylinder is 471 cm². The height of the cylinder is twice its radius. Find its volume using π = 3.14.
A cylindrical metal pipe is 2.5 m long. Its outer diameter is 10 cm and its inner diameter is 6 cm. Find the volume of metal used to make the pipe. Use π = 3.14.
A rectangular prism has dimensions in the ratio 1 : 2 : 3. Its total surface area is 352 cm². Find its volume.
A regular square pyramid has a total surface area of 384 cm² and a base edge of 12 cm. Find its volume.
A right circular cone has a total surface area of 96π cm² and a slant height of 10 cm. Find its volume.
The volume of a sphere is 972π cm³. A plane cuts the sphere at a distance of 4 cm from its center. Find the area of the resulting cross-section.
A regular square pyramid has a slant height of 15 cm and a base edge of 18 cm. Find its volume.
A pyramid has a square base of side 10 cm. A horizontal cross-section taken at a distance of 6 cm from the vertex has an area of 36 cm². Find the height of the pyramid.

The altitude of a right circular cone is 16 cm. A plane parallel to the base cuts the cone at a distance of 6 cm from the base. If the area of the resulting cross-section is 25π cm², find the volume of the original cone using π = 3.14.

A right circular cone has a volume of 401.92 cm³ and a base radius of 8 cm. A horizontal cross-section is taken at a distance of 3 cm from the vertex. Find the radius of this cross-section using π = 3.14.
A right circular cone has a lateral surface area of 204.1 cm² and a slant height of 13 cm. Find its volume using π = 3.14.
A frustum of a regular square pyramid has a lateral surface area of 600 cm² and a slant height of 12 cm. The upper base has a side of 8 cm. Find the side length of the lower base.
A frustum of a right circular cone has base radii of 9 cm and 3 cm and a height of 12 cm. Find its volume using π = 3.14.

A right circular cone of height 18 cm is cut by a plane parallel to the base at a distance of 6 cm from the vertex, forming a frustum. If the radius of the original cone is 12 cm, find the volume of the frustum using π = 3.14.
A frustum of a regular square pyramid has a height of 6 cm. The areas of the upper and lower bases are 36 cm² and 144 cm² respectively. Find its volume.
A frustum of a right circular cone has a slant height of 13 cm and base radii of 10 cm and 5 cm. Find its total surface area using π = 3.14.

A right circular cone is cut by a plane parallel to the base to form a frustum. The ratio of the height of the smaller cone to the height of the original cone is 2 : 5. The original cone has a radius of 10 cm. If the volume of the frustum is 156π cm³, find the height of the frustum.

A solid is made of a right circular cylinder of radius 6 cm and height 10 cm with a right circular cone of the same base radius and height 8 cm mounted on top. Find the total surface area of the solid using π = 3.14.
A solid consists of a hemisphere of radius 7 cm mounted on a cylinder of the same radius. If the total height of the solid is 21 cm, find its volume using π = 3.14.
From a cylindrical block of wood of radius 8 cm and height 15 cm, a cone of the same radius and height is scooped out. Find the volume of the wood remaining using π = 3.14.
A solid is formed by joining a cone to a hemisphere of the same radius. The total height of the solid is 17 cm and the radius is 6 cm. Find the volume using π = 3.14.

A rectangular prism of dimensions 12 cm × 12 cm × 20 cm has a cylindrical hole of radius 4 cm drilled through its entire height (the 20 cm dimension). Find the volume of the remaining solid using π = 3.14.

A composed solid consists of a cylinder of radius 5 cm and height 9 cm with a cone of the same radius on top and a hemisphere of the same radius at the bottom. The cone has a height of 12 cm. Find the total surface area of the solid using π = 3.14.
A conical tent of radius 5 m and height 12 m is made of canvas. Find the cost of the canvas required at Birr 180 per m² using π = 3.14.
A water tank in the shape of a frustum of a cone has base radii of 2 m and 1 m and a height of 3 m. How many liters of water can it hold? Use π = 3.14 and 1 m³ = 1000 L.
A metallic sphere of radius 6 cm is melted down and recast into a right circular cylinder of height 32 cm. Find the radius of the cylinder using π = 3.14.
A cylindrical can has a volume of 1570 cm³ and a height of 20 cm. Find its total surface area using π = 3.14.
A bucket in the shape of a frustum of a cone has a top radius of 10 cm, a bottom radius of 5 cm, and a slant height of 13 cm. Find its capacity in liters. Use π = 3.14 and 1 L = 1000 cm³.

A contractor builds a concrete pillar consisting of a cylinder of radius 0.7 m and height 3 m with a hemisphere of the same radius on top. Concrete costs Birr 3500 per m³. Find the total cost of the concrete needed using π = 3.14.
